response.reasoning: Step 1: Years of experience — The candidate has been working since ~2006 (freelance) or 2009 (first formal employment), giving 17–20 years of engineering work. The 15-year minimum is satisfied. Step 2: Education — Holds a BS in Computer Science (University of Bucharest) and a Bachelor's in Fine Arts/Computer Image Processing. No Master's or PhD, and nothing AI-specific at graduate level; the preferred qualification is unmet. Step 3: AI expertise — AI involvement appears exclusively in the most recent role (April 2025–present at re:cinq), covering MCP code generators and agentic workflow tooling. This is developer-tooling work, not strategic AI product development, recognized research, or demonstrated AI platforms at scale. No prior role shows meaningful AI or ML work. Step 4: Cloud expertise — AWS CDK appears once (Optilyz). No cloud platform strategy, cloud architecture at enterprise scale, or customer advisory experience is present. Step 5: Seniority and leadership — Titles are Senior Software Developer and Staff Software Engineer. The role is a Director-level IC expected to advise strategic customers, partner with product leadership, and inspire internal and external stakeholders. The candidate's profile is a capable senior/staff IC, not a Principal with enterprise-level advisory credibility. Step 6: Entrepreneurial background — GISCollective (open-source GIS platform, founded 2016, still active) and early freelance history partially satisfy the entrepreneurial signal. Step 7: Thought leadership — FOSS4G conference talk and community workshop facilitation show knowledge-sharing ability, but these are niche community contributions, not industry-recognized AI thought leadership. Overall: strong generalist engineer with very recent AI tooling exposure, but materially short on the AI depth, cloud strategy scope, and Director-level IC positioning the role demands.
strengths: 17–20 years of diverse engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure comfortably meets the 15-year minimum threshold
GISCollective founder (open-source geospatial platform, 2016–present) and early freelance work demonstrate a genuine builder and entrepreneurial mindset aligned with the preferred profile
Consistent knowledge-sharing through conference talks, multi-day workshops, and community coding sessions shows communication and mentoring ability relevant to the 'inspire others' responsibility
concerns: AI involvement is shallow and very recent (one role, April 2025–present), limited to developer tooling; no demonstrated deep AI/ML expertise, recognized AI products shipped at scale, or strategic AI advisory track record
Cloud experience is thin — one mention of AWS CDK at a product-feature level; no cloud platform strategy, cloud architecture design, or enterprise cloud advisory experience in evidence

Current and historical seniority (Staff/Senior Software Engineer) does not reflect the Director-level IC scope; no evidence of advising enterprise customers, shaping organizational AI strategy, or operating at the principal/executive influence level
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned, technically broad engineer with a genuine builder instinct and solid experience depth, but the Principal Engineer (AI Strategy) role requires a Director-level IC who is a recognized AI expert capable of advising enterprise customers and influencing cloud product strategy at scale. His AI experience is confined to a single recent role doing agentic developer tooling, with no history of building strategic AI products, no advanced AI-related degree, and no evidence of the enterprise advisory credibility or thought leadership the position demands.
